questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: In the context of the Protection of Human Rights Act (PHRA), 1993, how are 'Human Rights' defined and enforced in the Indian legal framework?

options.text: They are limited to the Fundamental Rights specifically enumerated in Part III of the Constitution of India.
They include rights relating to life, liberty, equality, and dignity embodied in International Covenants and enforceable by courts in India.
They refer to any right recognized by the United Nations General Assembly, regardless of their enforceability in Indian domestic courts.
They are rights defined by the NHRC through its own regulations and are advisory in nature for the judiciary.

questions.explanation: The PHRA defines human rights as rights relating to life, liberty, equality, and dignity of the individual. These must be either guaranteed by the Constitution or embodied in International Covenants and must be enforceable by courts in India. This ensures a statutory link between international standards and domestic judicial enforcement.
questions.takeaway: The definition of human rights under PHRA is broad but requires a nexus with either the Indian Constitution or enforceable international covenants.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: PHRA defines Human Rights as rights relating to life, liberty, equality and dignity of the individual. These rights are guaranteed by Constitution or embodied in International Covenants and are enforceable by courts in India.
